steps.title: Recognize slope-intercept form
steps.content: The given function is $f(x) = 3x - \frac{1}{4}$. This is already in slope-intercept form where $m = 3$ (the slope) and $b = -\frac{1}{4}$ (the y-intercept).

steps.title: Extract the y-intercept point
steps.content: In slope-intercept form, the constant term gives us the y-intercept point. Since $b = -\frac{1}{4}$, the y-intercept is $(0, -\frac{1}{4})$. That matches choice A — A is correct.
